topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification chimed, a bright, insistent sound that cut through the quiet hum of the library. My heart gave a sudden, startling leap against my ribs. I pulled out my phone, my fingers trembling slightly as I navigated to my social feed. There, in the grid of familiar profiles, was Professor Albright’s latest post—a picture of a sunset, captioned with a thoughtful quote about mentorship. My breath hitched. I stared at the screen, a slow, warm flush spreading across my cheeks. It felt like a secret shared, an acknowledgment that resonated deep within my chest. I reread the caption three times, a small, involuntary smile tugging at the corners of my mouth. A wave of pure, buoyant excitement washed over me, making the air feel lighter, almost fizzy. I couldn't stop the soft, delighted laugh that escaped me.

[story 2]

I was scrolling mindlessly through Instagram, zoning out during a particularly tedious lecture review. Then, there it was: a direct message from Professor Albright. It was a simple question about a paper, but the way he’d phrased it, the casual familiarity in his tone—it sent a jolt straight through me. My hands, resting on the desk, suddenly felt strangely warm. I leaned closer to the screen, my gaze locked on his profile picture. A bright, almost dizzying sensation bubbled up inside me, making my vision sharpen. I quickly typed a response, my thumbs moving with an uncharacteristic lightness. When I hit send, I didn't just feel pleased; I felt utterly radiant, as if I’d just won a secret lottery. I leaned back in my chair, letting the feeling settle, a deep, satisfying warmth spreading from my core outward.

[story 3]

It happened while I was checking my notifications after submitting an assignment. A new update from Professor Albright. He had tagged me in a story—a candid shot from his weekend hike, looking remarkably relaxed. My entire body seemed to vibrate with a sudden, intense energy. I dropped my pen; it rolled silently across the polished wood floor. I picked up my phone again, my movements becoming quick and almost jerky with anticipation. A wide, unrestrained grin stretched across my face, the kind that felt almost too big for my features. Every nerve ending buzzed with a thrilling current. It was an undeniable sense of validation, a perfect alignment of worlds, and I couldn't contain the sheer joy bubbling up inside me. I wanted to shout, but I managed only a soft, breathless chuckle.
